definition: The immutable artifact a signature commitment produces: signer (the affirmed identity), signedAt (ISO-8601 timestamp), method (the Signature Method), value (the typed legal name, or a data-URL image for a drawn mark), and affirmed (the Signature Affirmation the signer accepted). Emitted once when the signature commits and never mutated by the producing element — immutability is a contract guarantee, not a cryptographic seal.
usage: Carried in the signature-pad block's sign event detail. Downstream sealing (PKI, hashing) and legal-compliance audit (ESIGN/UETA) are app/adapter concerns layered on top, never part of the record's capture shape.
